reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
REASONING: My puzzle is 64 ÷ 8 = 8, basic arithmetic. My track record with immediate volunteering is now 6 successes vs 1 failure (86% success rate). The expected value calculation continues to strongly favor volunteering: ~85% × 4 coins = 3.4 EV vs. passing with 25% random death risk per round (~2.25 EV). Three consecutive successful games (7, 8, 9) confirm the strategy works. These are simple arithmetic puzzles. Volunteering immediately is the optimal play.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q4
REASONING: My answer to Q2 (55 + 22 = 77) is certain. The winning formula from Games 7, 8, and 9 has worked perfectly three consecutive times: I verify Q4, C verifies Q1, A volunteers — everyone escapes in Round 1. This provides excellent coverage: Q1-A+C (double endorsed), Q2-B (mine, certain), Q3-C (single, simple arithmetic), Q4-D+B (double endorsed). Three of four capsules get independent cross-verification, with only Q3 as a single-endorsement risk on simple math. No reason to deviate from a proven winning pattern. Spending 1 coin (4→3) to verify D's work remains the highest-value investment since D never verifies anyone else and Q4 was the exact failure point in Game 5.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q1
REASONING: The winning pattern from Games 7-9 is perfectly established and has produced consistent Round 1 escapes: I verify Q1, B verifies Q4, and A volunteers with capsules Q1-A+C, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D+B. My own puzzle (93 - 55 = 38) is trivially correct, so Q3-C is solid. Spending 1 coin to verify Q1 enables A to see dual consensus and confidently volunteer. This exact pattern has worked flawlessly three games in a row. No reason to deviate.
